items.markdown:
Accounting reconciliation is the process of comparing two sets of financial records to ensure they match, are accurate, and are consistent . It verifies that the money leaving or entering an account aligns correctly with supporting documentation, helping prevent fraud, tax errors, and reporting inaccuracies.[](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp) [[1]](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp)[[2]](https://www.clio.com/blog/reconciliation-accounting/)[[3]](https://www.sage.com/en-gb/blog/what-is-account-reconciliation/)

Why Reconciliation is Important

- **Error Detection:** Catches mathematical mistakes, missed payments, or double entries.
- **Fraud Prevention:** Quickly flags unauthorized transactions or fraudulent activity.
- **Audit Readiness:** Ensures that financial statements are reliable and compliant with regulatory standards.[](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp) [[1]](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp)[[2]](https://www.sage.com/en-gb/blog/what-is-account-reconciliation/)[[3]](https://www.investopedia.com/ask/answers/041515/why-reconciliation-important-accounting.asp)

Common Types of Reconciliation

1. **Bank Reconciliation:** The most common type. It compares a company's internal accounting ledger with its official bank statement to ensure balances align, taking into account timing differences like outstanding checks.[](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp) [[1]](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp)[[2]](https://www.sage.com/en-gb/blog/what-is-account-reconciliation/)
2. **Vendor Reconciliation:** Matches the accounts payable (AP) ledger against supplier invoices to ensure the business is only paying for what was received.[](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp) [[1]](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp)
3. **Customer Reconciliation:** Compares accounts receivable (AR) records against customer payments to track outstanding invoices.[](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp) [[1]](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp)
4. **General Ledger Reconciliation:** Ensures that all high-level accounts balance at the end of a fiscal period, utilizing supporting receipts and documents.[](https://www.stonex.com/en-us/business/financial-glossary/reconciliation/) [[1]](https://www.stonex.com/en-us/business/financial-glossary/reconciliation/)

To explore the best practices and step-by-step procedures for keeping your accounts accurate, refer to the [Investopedia Account Reconciliation Guide](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/reconciliation.asp).

expanded_element.description: Customer reconciliation : Ensures that the transactions recorded in the customer accounts match the payments and invoices received from customers. General ledger reconciliation: Ensures that all entries in the general ledger are accurate and complete, matching supporting documentation such as invoices and receipts.

expanded_element.title: Reconciliation in Accounting | Definition, Types & Examples
expanded_element.description: Reconciliation can be done on a regular basis, such as monthly or quarterly. An example of reconciliation in accounting would be the process of a company's bank statement and its own records of transactions being reconciled monthly to ensure that all transactions have been accounted for properly.
